response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced full-stack web and TypeScript developer, but fails nearly all mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years required, and neither was on Windows. He has no SQL Server or Oracle experience (his stack is MongoDB and MySQL), no fixed income or capital markets domain knowledge, and no investment banking background. The one adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was a fintech consumer investment startup using Node.js, not capital markets infrastructure. Optional skills like CI/CD and Java provide marginal positive signal but do not offset the core gaps.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development demonstrating strong engineering fundamentals and adaptability across domains
Solid CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development) matching an optional desirable skill
Java development background (NI, Altom) satisfies one of the optional desirable qualifications
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months total (NI LabWindows/CVI, 2014–2015) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years on Windows
Zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking — the role requires at least one of these domain areas
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is limited to MongoDB and MySQL, neither of which meets the mandatory SQL requirement
